title: Dense Fluffy Peruvian Basmati Rice Pilaf
description: Salty snappy crowd-pleaser Peruvian dish featuring basmati rice glaze pescatarian friendly served in banana-leaves.
instructions: Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add rice and stir to coat with oil and mix with onion and garlic.
Add cilantro ginger and aji amarillo pepper. Stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for 18-20 minutes or until water is absorbed.
While rice is cooking, prepare Peruvian glaze by mixing together 1/2 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of honey and 1 tablespoon of grated ginger in a small saucepan.
Bring glaze to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes or until glaze thickens slightly.
To serve, place a portion of rice on a banana leaf and brush with Peruvian glaze.
Fold banana leaf over the rice to form a package and serve.
